title: LocalStorage Array via eval()
description: DOM-based XSS that reads a value from localStorage and passes it into eval() inside an array literal context.
objectives: Trace how the 'items' key flows from localStorage.getItem() into eval().
Break out of the surrounding [ ... ] literal to execute arbitrary JavaScript.
Confirm the payload persists across reloads until localStorage is cleared.
Craft a payload that executes without breaking the downstream arr.join() call — understand why naive payloads throw TypeError.
hints: The value is inlined as eval('[' + storedValue + ']') — close the bracket, run your JS, then comment the trailing ']' out.
Reset the state with localStorage.removeItem('items') or localStorage.clear().
body:
LocalStorage -> eval() (array context)

Set localStorage.items to control the array contents.
